level_result_system.source_note: Each level has a report source and a door object for front-end inspection. Doors are internal navigation/output markers, not approval gates. Boss corrected on 2026-06-14 that Chapter 2 is Root Room; Mud Pit follows Root Room before Trunk Ascent. Boss selected current chapter display names on 2026-06-14: Spark Library, Root Room, Mud Pit, Trunk Ascent, The Name, Canopy Foundation, Wind Tunnel, Branchworks, Heavy Crown, Clean Sweep, Nestwatch, Seed Garden.

open_questions.question: Who would this help?
open_questions.weak_spot: Needs a little more natural detail from the user.

open_questions.question: What would a useful first version do?
open_questions.weak_spot: Needs a little more natural detail from the user.

open_questions.question: What part are you picturing most clearly right now?
open_questions.weak_spot: Needs a little more natural detail from the user.

open_questions.question: If this eventually made money, what feels like the most natural way that could happen?
Do you have any examples, references, notes, or past experiences that point toward this?
open_questions.weak_spot: No deep research or validated market proof in this dry run.

levels.boundary: Internal review only. No publishing, spending, credentials, webhooks, external accounts, backend storage, or bot authority changes.
levels.lovable_section: Saved ideas list opens a selected idea; the Library report action runs from the opened Idea Dashboard.

levels.report_summary: Capture and protect the baseline after Library: Echo perspective, Shield boundary pass, Ledger baseline record, and Chief root handoff before the idea enters the next chapter.
levels.boundary: Root Room preserves and closes the baseline only. It does not approve public action, spending, launch, deployment, live n8n expansion, credential use, runtime/config changes, source-truth rewrites, or bot authority changes.
levels.lovable_section: Root Room page / five root tunnels / completion report.

levels.report_summary: Pressure-test the Root Room baseline before Trunk Ascent begins. Name weak spots, proof needs, kill risks, and the stronger version.

should_include: weak assumptions and likely break points
stronger version to carry into Trunk Ascent

levels.boundary: Crossfire challenges the idea only. It does not reject ideas, override Boss, approve spending, approve launch, contact n8n, dispatch bots, or change runtime/config/authority.
levels.lovable_section: Mud Pit chapter card / pressure-test door until a dedicated Mud Pit page exists.

levels.report_summary: Prepare the project for R&D/product-direction work without losing the original clean packet, baseline, safety flags, or creator-control rules.
levels.lovable_section: Trunk Ascent review screen where Luma, Bloom, Vault, and Compass are shown as broad role-based contributors.

levels.report_summary: After Trunk research, treat The Name like a clean piece of paper: Moniker starts the project by naming the thing and setting URL direction early enough to guide logo, colors, font, brand tone, and later design work.
levels.n8n_anchor: 7.8. The Name: Prepare Project Name and URL Options
levels.lovable_section: The Name chapter card / naming door until a dedicated The Name page exists.

levels.report_summary: Turn Library, Trunk, The Name, Lantern, Compass, Moniker, Ledger, Shield, and Echo inputs into a clear project foundation before Branchworks explores build/design options.

should_include: strengths, weak spots, missing decisions, and assumptions
money, growth, design, and direction-continuity notes
Branchworks-ready first questions and do-not-drift boundaries

levels.boundary: Canopy prepares internal planning only. It does not approve build, launch, spending, public claims, account actions, legal/trademark claims, domain purchase, or bot authority changes.
levels.n8n_anchor: 8. Canopy Foundation: Prepare Rook Foundation Packet
levels.lovable_section: Canopy Foundation chapter card / Rook foundation door until a dedicated Canopy Foundation page exists.

levels.report_summary: Pressure-test the Canopy/Rook foundation for worthiness, scope strength, risk, and build readiness before Branchworks turns it into Tinker, Momma, and Bear prototype directions.

should_include: user value and audience confidence check
known risks, weak assumptions, and missing proof
Stagehand tightening or repair notes when needed
Branchworks-ready instructions for Tinker, Momma, and Bears

levels.boundary: Wind Tunnel is pre-build validation and strengthening only. It does not approve build execution, Lovable submission, deployment, public launch, spending, account access, backend setup, route/config/tool changes, final product selection, or bot authority changes.
levels.lovable_section: Wind Tunnel level card / worthiness door until a dedicated Wind Tunnel page exists.

levels.report_summary: Turn the Rook/Canopy foundation into Bear-ready experiment directions and a three-door Lovable preview concept while preserving closed gates.

should_include: Momma Bear master prompt preserving the three directions
Tinker final check result and closed-gate confirmation

levels.boundary: Branchworks prepares internal experiment/design direction only. It does not approve Lovable submission, build execution, deployment, public launch, spending, account access, backend setup, route/config/tool changes, final product selection, or bot authority changes.
levels.lovable_section: Branchworks chapter card / Tinker experiment door until a dedicated Branchworks page exists.

levels.report_summary: Turn Branchworks Bear directions and Tinker checks into one stronger prototype package, while preserving source choices and guarding against drift.
levels.boundary: Heavy Crown prepares an internal prototype package, Shield Final Boundary Pass, and next-level handoff only. It does not approve Lovable submission, build execution, deployment, public launch, spending, account access, backend setup, route/config/tool changes, live support activation, final product selection, or bot authority changes.
levels.n8n_anchor: 8.6. Heavy Crown: Prepare Weaver Prototype Package
levels.lovable_section: Heavy Crown chapter card / Weaver prototype door until a dedicated Heavy Crown page exists.

levels.report_summary: Have Ghost run end-stage simulations across the near-final Crown package / Da Crown Door experience before build handoff, owner review, public use, launch planning, or operational movement.
levels.boundary: Clean Sweep is simulation/reporting only. It does not approve Lovable submission, browser/computer-use automation, account access, production data use, public action, spending, deployment, code fixes, auto-merge, final product approval, route/config/tool changes, or bot authority changes.
levels.n8n_anchor: 8.7. Clean Sweep: Prepare Ghost Simulation Report
levels.lovable_section: Clean Sweep chapter card / Ghost simulation report until a dedicated Clean Sweep page exists.

levels.report_summary: Receive the swept project into its living home, define health and watch signals, keep it from going stale, and route the right helper when it needs care.

should_include: Anteater/Ant Watch Report input when available
project home/location and target surface status
forward growth opportunities and proposed additions
High Council review notes and routed fixes
version number update when meaningful changes are made

levels.boundary: Nest is project-home/watch/readiness guidance unless later approved as live operations. It does not approve cron automation, live support operations, support inboxes, public launch, spending, deployment, customer promises, production monitoring, account/channel/runtime/config changes, final product approval, or bot authority changes.
levels.n8n_anchor: 8.8. Nestwatch: Prepare Ward Living Project Home
levels.lovable_section: Nestwatch chapter card / Ward project-home door until a dedicated Nestwatch page exists.

levels.report_summary: Turn the Living Seed Packet into a practical Seed Launch Packet so the strongest seeds can work at their best ability after the project has a home in Nest.

should_include: Senior Seed foundation notes for each baby seed
future Analytics/Stats placeholder only when clearly marked or backed by real data
Seed Admin Page sections and field notes

levels.boundary: Seed prepares launch/growth/distribution guidance plus internal/admin Seed Page and DaBotTree Admin Page concepts only. It does not approve public launch, posting, ads, spending, account access, email/message sends, customer promises, legal/commercial claims, automation, deployment, live support, final launch authority, public exposure of internal seed data, or bot authority changes.
levels.n8n_anchor: 8.9. Seed Garden: Prepare Bloom Seed Launch Packet
levels.lovable_section: Seed Garden chapter card / Bloom launch packet door until a dedicated Seed Garden page exists.
